mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the cross-sectional IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and sustains positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any rolling 3-year period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, industrial production growth decelerates while capacity utilization peaks, signaling impending supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Industrial production deceleration does not precede negative 20-day equity returns in more than 60% of observed late-cycle episodes, or the forward return differential between decelerating and accelerating phases is less than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above a 2.5 percent annualized pace, inflation expectations trigger premature rate hike fears, causing immediate equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI readings above the threshold consistently yield positive or neutral 5-day forward returns, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 45% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent reflects underlying economic momentum that directly supports corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth above 2.0% fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth acceleration and unemployment rate declines confirm labor market validation, which reduces earnings volatility and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint payroll-unemployment signal does not produce a statistically significant positive alpha (t-stat < 1.65) over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are indistinguishable from the baseline index.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, initial retail sales rebounds signal pent-up demand release, which rapidly lifts consumer discretionary earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales rebounds in recovery periods fail to outperform non-recovery periods by at least 1.0% over 5-day horizons, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward validation.
